name: Stage 6a: Zwift Games: Epiloch
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: INC Relentless Intervals
description:
This is a Team Time Trial Training session. Expect a hard interval session. The front of this ride will suit Category A riders, however other categories are welcome and encouraged to join and complete the workout in your own gruppettos.

These are no sweep training rides; if you drop from the group, simply continue the workout on your own or with others of a similar pace.
This is an Innovation Cycling Training Ride Event – all riders are welcome. These events are put together to push you further than before, if you are feeling fresh hop on the front, if you need rest you can draft!

name: Cycle Nation Joburg Sunrise
description:
Welcome to the Cycle Nation Joburg Sunrise ride. Join us for an easy morning coffee ride aimed at Zwifters looking for an easy paced morning group ride. All riders are welcome to join the event and enjoy the friendly atmosphere. Please follow the ride leaders instructions and try group around the beacon. Most importantly this isn't a race and we ask that you respect the pace set by the ride leader which will allow for an enjoyable group ride experience for all.

Join our Facebook Group: https://bit.ly/38TTtAh
Join our Zwift Team: https://bit.ly/3D2Q4zL
Join our Strava Club: https://bit.ly/3pEDkFM
Twitter: https://bit.ly/3n4uhft
Instagram: https://bit.ly/3pEsdN6

name: Team Italy - Smile! It's Tuesday Ride
description:
Join the Team Italy Smile! It's Tuesday Ride each week, we’ll roll as a group at a moderate 2.5-3 w/kg pace. The course varies each week, with a range of undulating routes. These rides are perfect towards Zwifters that are looking for a varied content one hour session that can give them a full 5 zone workout. Draft in the group and stay zone 1-3, or move to the front and join the sprints for zones 4 and 5 for short bursts.

Our sweeper team will help you, if requested. Experience with group rides is of advantage - we will use the power of the group to go fast, please follow the Leader (yellow) istructions, the fence is the red barrier behind the leader, if you overtake for 60 seconds it will kick out of the ride, so respect the Leader pace, if you have extra watts you can help our sweepers Team. 

Please Add the tag Ita into your surname. When you finished the ride You will earn the Team Italy Jersey, check your Zwift Garage. If you like our events join our Companion Club Team Italy,  Facebook Group https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftitaly  Telegram https://t.me/ZwiftTeamItaly
#TeamItaly #Respect4Leader

name: Vikings Valhalla Tuesday Climb
description:
Welcome to the ‘Vikings Valhalla Tuesday Climb’. Are you ready to do some climbing at a pace that suits you and have a great time as well? We will supply quality leads and sweeps, all you need to do is be ready to sweat! We have two groups to accommodate different rider levels, if you find yourself in "no mans land" jump in and help sweep to get that little extra burn. Our rides are very inclusive with a "no one gets dropped" approach. We encourage riders to respect the leader's pace and ride with the yellow beacon. This is NOT A RACE! As mentioned, stronger riders are encouraged to fall back and help sweep the riders struggling in/off the back. If you are looking for a fun ride with a great group of people then, this is the
perfect ride for you!

For more information about our rides go to: https://www.facebook.com/groups/503388660165279/

eventSubgroups.name: Vikings Valhalla Tuesday Climb (D)
eventSubgroups.description: #doubledraft

name: Hercules - Breakfast Club
description:
Start your day right with the Breakfast Club — a super friendly and inclusive group ride on Zwift! We ride with the rubber band enabled, so no one gets dropped, and everyone stays together no matter the pace.

Expect great chat, good vibes, quizzes, fun facts and a welcoming atmosphere for all riders, whether you're new to Zwift or a seasoned pro.

Please join our Zwift Club at https://www.zwift.com/uk/clubs/d6581e47-deb2-46ed-a278-5777d5d2fd0f/home

Please join our Facebook Group at https://www.facebook.com/groups/2606407819659263/

Please join us online at https://herculesrunningandtri.com/

Please join us on Strava at https://www.strava.com/clubs/herculesrunningandtriclub

If you would like to own our famous Pink & Black Club Kit in real life, please order at https://tinyurl.com/herculestrirun

name: EVO CC Vortex Interval Ride [~3-3.8w/kg avg]
description:
Welcome to the Evolution Cycling Club [EVO] interval session. These rides are geared towards Zwifters that are looking for a varied content, one hour session that can give them a full 5 zone workout. Draft in the group and stay zone 1-3, or move to the front and join the sprints for zones 4 and 5 for short bursts. Average for this ride will be mid to high Category B but it may vary based upon course/leader. There will be keepers at the back, and they will try hard to save dropped riders. The group is based upon high social interaction so bring your best banter. Use the mobile app or a keyboard to communicate in-ride.

#evo

name: EVO CC Flux Ride [1.8 - 2.0w/kg avg]
description:
This is a Evolution Cycling Club [EVO] Social Event and ride for all. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that all riders respect the pace set by the leader (Yellow Beacon). We may include intervals over and below the ride average advertised power range, but we will allow enough recovery and time to regroup after every effort. This is not a race, so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong we would welcome any help offered by stronger riders to bring any dropped riders back to the yellow beacon. We welcome good chat and a social atmosphere for new and experienced Zwifters alike, everyone is welcome.

#evo

name: Team Valhalla Early/Late Everybody's In Spin
description:
Welcome everyone short, tall, large, small and in between to our Tuesday Team Valhalla Early/Late Everybody’s In Spin.

This is D category GROUP ride with the aim to ride as a group at a pace that is inclusive and empathetic to everyone from the seasoned rider to the first timer. The advertised pace is 1.6-2W/KG.

Do you have tired legs or are you early morning pre-espresso fatigued - then this is the ride for you.

Is it a late night or afternoon and you are getting ready for supper or bed - then this is the ride for you.

Are you new to Zwift or Group Rides and worry about getting dropped or worry about a Lead that doesn’t care to help those out back? - then this is the ride for you.

This is a “no-drop” “GROUP” ride around D or Recovery pace so if you do find yourself off the back then just shout out early and there will always be a friendly face to keep you company and help get you back to the Yellow Beacon. All you have to bring is your smile. If you are a stronger rider looking for an extra challenge we would encourage you to help sweep riders off the back.

The 6 goals of this group ride are simple: 1) ride near/with the yellow beacon, 2) enjoy each other’s company/make new friends, 3) be positive, 4) engage in the banter, 5) Big the Blob and 6) HAVE FUN.

We want this ride and all our Vikings and Valhalla Rides to be a great ride experience for Everyone!!

Please check out our Facebook group at: https://www.facebook.com/groups/503388660165279/?ref=share

eventSubgroups.name: Team Valhalla Early/Late Everybody's In Spin (D)

name: SZR Early Birdies
description:
Welcome to SZR Early birdies

This is a group ride with the focus on remaining together. You should aim to sit between the yellow and red beacon. Follow ride leader instructions at all times.

We will ride at an average pace of 2.5 w/kg prior to any race finish. The race finish is at the discretion of the ride leader and will vary in length dependent on the ride.

If there are any sprints allowed during the ride please regroup on the yellow beacon immediately after.

After hills we will focus on getting the back of the group together with the front.

#SZR

name: 3R VOLT Interval Ride [~2.6-3.2 w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. 3R VOLT runs five days a week and is designed to provide a solid and fun training platform for riders.

Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 2.8-3.2 w/kg.

The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides and training needs, the following is a typical week:
Monday – After what has hopefully been a fun backed weekend on the bike, this our recovery session
Tuesday – Tends to be more of a classic interval session, riders usually finish with an average of around 3.0w/kg
Wednesday – We take to the hills and one of our tougher sessions with riders averaging around 3.2w/kg
Thursday – is our no red beacon ride and again tough interval sets and riders finishing around 3.2w/kg
Friday - is a little different, we don't take ourselves too seriously and often play a variety of games and quizzes along side the intervals and riders end up with around 2.8-3.0w/kg

The rides are typically around an hour long, but some may take up to 75mins with the majority finishing with either a ramp or a mini race.

The group welcomes rider interaction through messaging and the VOLT discord server and features a friendly bunch of regulars to which newcomers, and riders stepping up from lower intensity rides, are always welcome. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/

This group ride is not a race so. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise.

name: 3R Octane Interval Ride [~3.2-3.6 w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. 3R Octane runs five days a week and is designed to provide a solid and fun training platform for riders.

Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 3.2-3.8 W/kg.

The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides, including weekly recovery, or transitional, rides which are usually on a Monday. Short, high intensity intervals, longer sweetspot intervals, flying laps, long and short climbs and exploring little used routes all feature.

The rides are typically around an hour long, but some can be up to two hours long, and all end with a mini race.

The Group welcomes rider interaction through messaging and the Octane discord server and features a super bunch of regulars to which newcomers, and riders stepping up from lower intensity rides, are always welcome. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/

#Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise #3r

eventSubgroups.name: 3R Octane Interval Ride [~3.2-3.6 w/kg avg] (B)
eventSubgroups.description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. 3R Octane runs five days a week and is designed to provide a solid and fun training platform for riders.

Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 3.2-3.8 W/kg.

The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides, including weekly recovery, or transitional, rides which are usually on a Monday. Short, high intensity intervals, longer sweetspot intervals, flying laps, long and short climbs and exploring little used routes all feature.

The rides are typically around an hour long, but some can be up to two hours long, and all end with a mini race.

The Group welcomes rider interaction through messaging and the Octane discord server and features a super bunch of regulars to which newcomers, and riders stepping up from lower intensity rides, are always welcome. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/

#Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise #3r

name: 3R SPARK Steady State Ride [~2.0-2.2 w/kg avg]
description: This is a 3R Group Ride Event (not a race). 3R Spark runs five days a week and is designed to be an accessible and sociable training session, aimed primarily at riders in Cat-D. Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 2.0-2.5 W/kg. The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides. Mondays and Tuesdays are our entry-level steady-state rides, where we aim to hold a steady 2.0-2.2 throughout. Thursdays and Fridays are our interval sessions, designed to help Cat D riders push themselves a little further. For interval sessions we aim to average between 2.2-2.5 for the session, but there will be times above and below. Thursday and Friday sessions often include an optional mini-race at the end. Wednesday is the Taylor Zwift Fan Club ride, a led by the hosts of the popular podcast of the same name – expect a sociable steady-state session with lots to keep you entertained! The TZFC ride is livestreamed at twitch.tv/TaylorZwiftFC. Rides are typically around an hour long. Spark is a great introduction to group rides on Zwift, we are a friendly and supportive group who love to see one another succeed. We always have a sweeper (red beacon) whose wheel you can grab if you get dropped. There’s always plenty of good chat, via in-ride messages or on Discord. Come and join us! Join the 3R Facebook group for more info: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team 3R #3R #RideRaceRaise

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

eventSubgroups.name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro (A)
NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro (B)

name: Zwift Beginners Weekly Group Workouts
description: Zwift Beginners is a welcoming place for all Zwift and cycling newbies looking to get in a group workout and find community! No pace is too slow, all questions are welcome and Ride Ons are given freely! These workouts will run Mondays and Wednesdays every 3 hours and be no more than 30 minutes (short and sweet). They are designed to give you a taste of what is possible on Zwift, nothing too hard or intimidating. All workouts are banded which means no matter what your level, we will all stick together! See you soon! Join the Zwift Beginner Club here https://www.zwift.com/.../b355516b-0136-45f8-ae6e.../join

eventSubgroups.name: Zwift Beginners Weekly Group Workouts (E)
eventSubgroups.description: A sweet spot with bursts effort variation that keeps you on the edge from start to finish. Riding a wave takes focus and control, can you get to the finish of this one without falling off?

name: Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This final session brings it all together. Two long tempo efforts build strong, sustainable endurance and reward the work you’ve done. Settle into a steady rhythm, stay composed, and finish the Alpecin–Premier Tech Training Camp strong!

name: GXY SURGE [1.6-2.5wkg] – CAT D
description:
Welcome to GXY Surge!
 
Different ride leaders will target varying effort levels each day, yet still bring you home well within the advertised power!
 
The ride is looking to push Cat Ds and low Cat Cs that little further all within a manageable 50 minute ride.  Please respect the leader’s pace and watch your fitness develop!
 
GXY are a friendly and supportive club who strive to help others succeed.  We are looking forward to riding with you.
 
If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or both, please come and join ideally via our Facebook link or by the email below:
 
https://www.facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/
https://www.strava.com/clubs/989894
https://www.instagram.com/galaxycyclingclub
 
Email:

eventSubgroups.name: GXY SURGE [1.6-2.5wkg] – CAT D (D)
eventSubgroups.description:
This is a GXY CC group ride (not a race). GXY Solar Surge is designed to be a sociable training session so come
warmed up ready to work! 

Different ride leaders will target varying effort levels taking riders through ramps, pyramids, sprints and sweet spot training with an occasional recovery ride thrown in forbalance.

The ride is aimed at pushing CAT D and low CAT C riders that little bit further all within a 50 min workout.
We recover EASY and we work HARD! Respect the leader’s pace which will change subject to the
workload, follow the session, work as a team together and watch your fitness develop.

GXY CC are a friendly and supportive team who strive to help others succeed.

Discover more about GXY CC by visiting https://www.Facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/

name: Stage 1: Zwift Games: Kaze Kicker
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

name: Mountain Massif Group Run
description:
Join Mountain Massif for some relaxed group runs. The goal is to simply complete the route and collect the badge.

More information:
For more information check out the Mountain Massif website www.mountainmassif.com, follow on Instagram, Mountain_Massif and join our Zwift Club, by clicking the link below
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b639575d-930f-4de6-a79d-bea3313350bc/join

name: Hercules - Breakfast Club
description:
Join our Breakfast Club 5k - a super friendly and inclusive group run on Zwift!

You can walk, jog or race, everyone is welcome.

Expect great chat, good vibes, and a welcoming atmosphere for all runners, whether you're new to Zwift or a seasoned pro.

Please join our Zwift Club at https://www.zwift.com/uk/clubs/d6581e47-deb2-46ed-a278-5777d5d2fd0f/home

Please join our Facebook Group at https://www.facebook.com/groups/2606407819659263/

Please join us online at https://herculesrunningandtri.com/

Please join us on Strava at https://www.strava.com/clubs/herculesrunningandtriclub

If you would like to own our famous Pink & Black Club Kit in real life, please order at https://tinyurl.com/herculestrirun

You can walk, jog or race, everyone is welcome.

Expect great chat, good vibes, and a welcoming atmosphere for all runners, whether you're new to Zwift or a seasoned pro.

name: Compass Community Cycling Thriving Tuesday
description:
Join our social group ride on Zwift — steady effort, good company, and just enough challenge to keep it interesting.

We’ll ride together through a mix of intervals: some short and sharp, others longer and steadier. It’s about turning the pedals, keeping each other going, and sharing the miles.

Each session usually runs two weeks in a row so you can ride it once, then ride it again and notice the difference. Little by little, you’ll find the effort feels smoother, the breathing steadier, the legs that bit stronger.

The chat is open, the music is loud, and there’s always room for some laughs along the way. Drop into the conversation, share how it’s going, or just sit in and spin — it’s your ride.

Recovery isn’t just for the legs. It’s a mental reset, too.





⚠️ Important note
• This is a social group ride, not a formal coaching session.
• Everyone rides at their own pace and you join at your own risk. Please ride within your personal limits and listen to your body.
• The leader and club are volunteers and accept no responsibility for injury, illness, or equipment issues arising from participation.

Show up. Spin steady. Grow stronger together. See you on the start line.

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: NGNM UNITED WE ARE Women's Workout Series
description:
Join our United We Are 5-Month Women’s Training Plan — a progressive program to get you ready for any real-life cycling challenge, from NGNM's United We Are Tour to gravel events, granfondos, or your next big goal.

You’ll build endurance, strength, and climbing power through 14 weeks of structured base work + 5 weeks of focused climbing training.

Train with **Mim Taylor - 2025 UK National Gravel Champion & UCI Worlds multi-podium athlete -** and Milly De Mori, NGNM’s founder.
You show up. We guide the way.
Because training solo is tough — but with United We Are, everything gets easier. 💪

⚡️🚴‍♀️ Event Details:

Workouts: < 1 hour

Community: We stay together—whatever your watts or FTP

Chat: Join us on Discord https://discord.gg/wywQsUaRYX
 or Zwift Companion's chat for support & motivation

💥 BONUS: Unlock NGNM’s exclusive WRL Kit on Zwift!

👉 Join NGNM’s United We Are Cycling Club on Companion:
https://www.zwift.com/eu/clubs/19908def-e39c-466b-9737-4db8bb40e503/join

—————
About No Gods No Masters® (NGNM) — Smart Cycling Apparel for Women
NGNM is more than a cycling brand: it’s a movement empowering women through style, performance, and freedom on&off the bike. Alongside high-end apparel, NGNM runs United We Are—exclusive road and gravel events across Italy and Europe, designed to bring women riders together on unforgettable adventures.
https://nogodsnomasters.life/pages/united-we-are-events

Order the RL of the NGNM jersey on Zwift here:
https://nogodsnomasters.life/collections/ngnm-x-zwift

eventSubgroups.name: NGNM UNITED WE ARE Women's Workout Series (E)

name: Tango Tuesday
description: The QueenBees are a group of women who share a love of cycling. Just as a hive works together to bring out the best in every member of the community, so do we. We strive to support, empower and encourage women to live their best lives, no matter where they are on their life, cycling or fitness journey. Join us for a social ride, all abilities welcome pace will be around 1.8 – 2.2wkg. During the ride try to stay with the yellow lead beacon and follow instructions from your ride leader.

name: INC Relentless Intervals
description:
Suitable for A riders as a base session, or B & C riders wanting to push themselves.

Examples of the drills are – Z2 with 15sec Z5/6 bursts every 6minutes, alternating Z2/Z3 in various durations, Z2 - Z4 ramps of decreasing duration and many other sessions.  

All sessions will be around 60-90mins and will include a warm up so just grab your bottles and head straight to the pen.

This is an Innovation Cycling Training Ride Event – all riders are welcome. These events are put together to push you further than before, if you are feeling fresh hop on the front, if you need rest surf the draft. This training ride is part of the INC RELENTLESS training rides that happen daily with each day focusing on a different area of improvement. 

Tips: Use the draft to rest and ensure you do not lose the draft.

name: Bikealicious Rubberband Joy Ride
description: This is a weekday ride that rides for 90 mins and for no less than 20 miles. So grab your bottles and trainer, let's ride! All riders are welcome to ride with us. This ride is put together to push you further than before and build your endurance. if you are feeling strong, push to the front and drag the group. If not, just sit in the peloton.

eventSubgroups.name: Bikealicious Rubberband Joy Ride (E)
eventSubgroups.description: This is a weekday ride that rides for 90 mins and for no less than 20 miles. So grab your bottles and trainer, let's ride! All riders are welcome to ride with us. This ride is put together to push you further than before and build your endurance. if you are feeling strong, push to the front and drag the group. If not, just sit in the peloton.

name: Bens Bash
description:
Training ride open to all. The aim of these rides is to provide a regular training ride for all to join in, riding at a steady pace that for some will feel challenging but over time you will get fitter and more comfortable.

Be mindful of your pace and try to keep the group together rather than just ride off on your own - look at this as a way to improve your fitness as well as pick up some skills around riding with others. 

Try not to ride over 3.5 w/kg but if you are feeling frisky - feel free to open the taps with 10 mins to go. 

We will vary the routes so check back regularly.

name: SZR Sunrise Ride
description:
Welcome to SZR Sunrise

C and D Ride
Tempo ride with Intervals
Sprints and intervals will be added at the discretion of the leader.
We will at times ride above and below the advertised pace, please don’t feel the need to tell the leader they will be aware but the ride average will be as advertised.
This is NOT a recovery ride, we work hard and work as a team.

E Ride
The focus is on the Group and keeping together while having fun to make the time fly fast!
The ride tempo is shown next to the ride category, this is rider weight / gender dependant.
Note that the average is for the ride prior to any optional faster finish (with a beacon).
The ride leader will aim (prior to any faster finish) to ride mid parameters after a short warmup period.
There might be slight variations above or below the average during the ride.


NB:
Please always respect the ride leader and follow his / her instructions.
The ride tempo is shown next to the ride category, this is rider weight / gender dependant.

name: Le Col Sessions Weekday Wind On
description:
A weekly ride for regular Le Col Sessions riders and newcomers alike. 

These sessions are inspired by our normal workout sessions and offer riders the chance to complete a more sustainable aerobic effort ahead of the more intense Wednesday session.

These sessions have been designed by ex-professional cyclist, Chris Opie, just like the rides Chris leads 3 times each week in the summer months, the sessions are designed to help you improve your cycling fitness. 
 
The Le col Cycling Club Community Sessions are the perfect opportunity to enjoy a structured workout and social interaction through the summer months. These rides will often be attended and led by our regular Le Col Sessions members 
 
Train towards challenges, QOMs, KOMs, distance goals, participation goals and build your Zwift XP in good company. 
 
Enjoy the ride? Why not join us at our Zwift Club, Le Col Cycling Club.

eventSubgroups.name: Le Col Sessions Weekday Wind On (E)

name: PAOLO BARBERA THE STAB
description:
Please visit www.heycoach.it or contact me 

Coach Paolo Barbera is a Certified Triathlon and Ironman Coach and the founder of Active Kids, a sporting clinic to help children and adults in the prevention and treatment of obesity through healthy nutrition and tailored training programmes.

Paolo is an experienced triathlete and cyclist. In 2017 he opened an innovative endurance training centre in milan, Tri60, specialized in indoor cycling.

It has been proven that Indoor cycling plays a key role to build strength, resistance and improve overall fitness and technical skills.

Paolo’s workouts are designed to mix different training zones aiming to maximize results in a very short time. Athletes following his workouts, at the right intensity and at the right cadence, have achieved surprising results.

Paolo’s indoor cycling workouts have different lengths to meet every athlete needs.

In the last ten years Paolo has developed very successful training programs for triathletes and cyclists

IT IS NOT HOW YOU START BUT HOW YOU FINISH!

GROUP WORKOUTS

Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normaL event and once the event starts, workout mode begins. Everyone stays together regardless of power output. We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests to face every workout at the right difficulty level.

name: Morning Free-Hisp
description:
Hispano, you’ll never ride alone!
There’s no distance long enough, there’s no challenge though enough, if you roll next to friends and Zwifters from around the globe.
Ready for a ride with us?
Join Us!
#KEEPtheKIT
WEB: https://hispclub.es
TELEGRAM: https://t.me/HISPCLUB
Ride On!

Un Hispano nunca rodará solo.
No hay distancia que no se pueda recorrer, ni meta que no se pueda alcanzar, si se rueda en buena compañía junto a ciclistas de todo el mundo.
¿Te vienes de paseo?
#QUEDATEelMAILLOT

name: Stage 2: Zwift Games: Hudson Hustle | Advanced
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

eventSubgroups.name: Stage 2: Zwift Games: Hudson Hustle | Advanced (A)

name: PAOLO BARBERA & SCANNELLATORI SERIALI WORKOUT
description:
"Scannellatori Seriali" is a large community of cyclists, triathletes, and runners. We invite you to join our community by following our Instagram page at https://www.instagram.com/scannellatori_seriali/ or contacting us on Facebook.
 
Coach Paolo Barbera (Please visit www.heycoach.it )   is a Certified Triathlon and Ironman Coach and the founder of Active Kids, a sporting clinic to help children and adults in the prevention and treatment of obesity through healty nutrition and tailored training programs

Paolo is an experienced thriathlete and cyclist, in 2017 opened an endurance training center in milan, Tri60, specilized in indoor training.

In the last ten years Paolo has developed training programs for triathletes, runners and cyclists. Indoor training plays a key role to control pace, cadence and mental strenght.

Paolo’s workouts are designed to mix different training zones aiming to maximize results in a very short time. Paolo’s coachees by following his workouts, at the right intensity, have achieved challenging results.

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: PACK Run Social
description:
PACK is well known for being one of the best social group rides on Zwift. But did you know that some Packers also like to...run?! That's right! You can now join your favorite group as we put down our bikes and pick up our running shoes.

The PACK social run is a 30 minute, relaxed pace 5k with several pace groups to fit your needs. Please choose the pace group you are most likely to stick with. This is NOT a race.

All runners are welcome to join the event and enjoy the friendly supportive atmosphere.

Facebook Group: #zwiftpack
https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftpack/

Discord Voice Chat:
https://discord.gg/cce7ers

#pack

name: Tous ensemble en bleu
description:
Le mode élastique favorise la cohésion au sein du groupe, permettant à chacun d’exprimer pleinement ses compétences. Ainsi, les défis ne deviennent plus des obstacles, mais plutôt des occasions de partage convivial et de plaisir collectif, transformant l’effort en une expérience enrichissante.

Discutez sur Discord /  Discuss on Discord: https://discord.gg/HgFb6SThT
Notre Facebook: https://m.facebook.com/groups/907351331715445

"Please add “(Gang)” at the end of your username in your Zwift profile.   Merci de mettre (Gang) à la fin de votre pseudo dans votre profil zwift 
"
Rubberband mode encourages group cohesion, allowing everyone to fully express their abilities.      Challenges are no longer obstacles, but opportunities for friendly sharing and collective enjoyment, turning effort into a truly rewarding experience.

name: TBR Sprinters paradise team intervals
description:
Work in with your group to recover, regroup and smash the all the sprints.
Remember to bring the bunch back together after each interval.

Sprint efforts:
1) Rev Castle Sprint Distance: 3.1km Length: 300m
2) Alley Sprint Distance: 4.6km Length: 500m
3) Railway Sprint Distance: 7.9km Length: 600m
Lap 2
4) Rev Tower Sprint Distance: 13.6km Length: 300m
5) Rev Castle Sprint Distance: 15.5km Length: 300m
6) Alley Sprint Distance: 17.0km Length: 500m
7) Railway Sprint Distance: 20.2km Length: 600m
Between after each effort allow re-grouping of all riders including those that continued at 1.8-2w/kg. This will allow enough recovery and help prepare for the next effort.

#tbr

name: Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This final session brings it all together. Two long tempo efforts build strong, sustainable endurance and reward the work you’ve done. Settle into a steady rhythm, stay composed, and finish the Alpecin–Premier Tech Training Camp strong!

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Champs Elysees
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Elastic social ride
description:
Soyez les bienvenu(e)s dans le ride du club BIKES (tag : BIKES)
But : le mode elastic permet de rester ensemble quelque soit les watts développés. Les plus faibles sont assurés de rester avec le leader !
Ce n’est pas une course.
Rejoins-nous ! ajoute le tag BIKES à ton pseudo Zwift et rejoins le club BIKES FRANCE
sur Companion

Discutez sur Discord: https://discord.gg/a8wYvgw
Notre Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZwiftBikes
Rejoignez-nous : https://www.bikesfrance.com/
Welcome to the BIKES club ride (tag: BIKES)

JOIN OUR NEW CLUB for non-french riders on Companion : club BIKES WORLD,
English spoken !
The idea is to bring together riders from all over the world, no matter where you come from, no matter your level, no matter if you are already in a club or not ; just have fun !

GOAL: Elastic mode allows everyone to stay together regardless of the watts generated. The weakest riders are guaranteed to stay with the leader!
This is not a race.
shortDescription: roulez ensemble sans jamais se séparer

name: Team Italy Coffee Italian Ride
description:
Se cerchi la giusta compagnia per prepararti al fine settimana sei nel posto giusto.
Lo scopo della COFFEE ITALIAN RIDE è quello di stare tutti insieme, sfruttare la scia del gruppo e fare due chiacchiere al ritmo medio di 2.0 w/kg per 60 minuti, il late join è attivo per 30 minuti.
Questa NON è una gara.
Segui le istruzioni dei leader(giallo) per un gruppo compatto.
Ricordati di non superare la fence (barra rossa) altrimenti verrai eliminato.
Partecipa a questa ride per sbloccare la divisa del team italy.
BUONA PEDALATA A TUTTI
TEAM ITALY organizza una vasta gamma di eventi, tutti con le seguenti regole:
-in una pedalata di categoria D, alcuni ciclisti sono principianti, segui le istruzioni del
leader (logo giallo) che appaiono come testo al centro e tieni presente che il ritmo sarà di
circa 2 wkg
-Il valore WKG sarà diverso per tutti voi, il motivo principale sarà il peso, seguito dalla
capacità di utilizzare la scia e l'equipaggiamento della vostra bici in gioco
-abilitare i messaggi nelle opzioni zwift, fondamentali per una buona esperienza
-le pedalate della categoria D sono facili per un ciclista con C alto, 3wkg ftp, impegnative per un C basso, 2.5 wkg ftp, estreme per un ciclista D con 2 wkg FTP, lo stesso per le
corse C, facili per un A, 4wkg ftp, impegnative per B 3.2 wkg ftp, ed estreme per C con 2.5
wkg ftp.


Are you looking for the perfect companion and you are training for the weekend? This is the right place.
Coffee Italian Ride's goal is to stay together, enjoy the group draft and a bit of easy chat at 2.0 w/kg for 90 minutes, late join is enabled for 30 minutes.
This is NOT A RACE.
Follow the leaders' instructions (yellow beacons) for keeping the group tight.
Remember the Fence is KILLER, 60 seconds over and you'll be out of the event.
Join this ride to unlock the Team Italy Jersey.
TEAM ITALY organizes a wide range of events, all agreed to the following rules:
- into a D category ride, some riders are beginners, follow the leader instructions (yellow
beacon) that appear as text in the middle and keep in mind that the pace will be around 2
wkg
-WKG value will be different for all of you, the main reason will be weight, followed by the
capacity to use the draft and your ingame bike equipment
- enable the messages into zwift options, fundamental for a great experience
- D category's ride are easy for a high C rider, 3wkg ftp, challenging for a low C, 2.5 wkg
ftp, extreme for a D rider with 2 wkg FTP, same for C rides, easy for an A, 4 wkg ftp,
challenging for B's, 3.2 wkg ftp and extreme for C's 2.5 wkg ftp.
- if you have any suggestions please let us know
#TeamItaly #JerseyUnlock #respect4leader
CONTACTS//CONTATTI

name: TEZH Racing | Endurance Ride
description:

name: AHDR Ladies Cake Ride p/b Liv
description:
Come along and join the ladies of AHDR, in a sub 2w/kg group ride, in the famous AHDR format. We will ride as a group with the aim of keeping everyone together, until the last 5km, when it is time for our optional ‘After Party’ race, chase or cruise. It’s the perfect way to get a taste of racing!

Please join https://www.facebook.com/groups/AHDRL to keep up to date with future rides and results.

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: WKG ROULEURS DOWN UNDER
description:
WKG Rouleurs Down Under will be a one hour ride over varied terrain where the group works together to improve their sustained efforts and team work. Beacon average pace will be 2-2.3 on the flat and 2.5-3 on the climbs. WKG Sweepers will help riders that become detached from the group as long as you make the effort with them.

Please check out WKG on Facebook for our events and community info.

#wkg

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Glasgow Crit Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Velos Sunny Side Up
description:
Time flies when you are having fun, but this is not a race, we just want to enjoy your company. Come and join team Velos for a D paced beginner friendly social spin for 60 minutes. Please pace with the yellow beacon at 1.5 - 1.8 w/kg. It’s a group ride so we encourage lots of chatter, maybe test you with some trivia questions and give you the opportunity to show off your pacing skills. Shout outs are given to those who catch the eye of the yellow beacon for riding close. Our supportive red beacon team will help any riders who drop behind to pace back to the yellow beacon if requested. Feel free to join in with the on screen chat, test your legs on a sprint or two or drop and help sweep at the back if your legs are strong. The fence may be used to encourage riders to work together and maximise the draft. Late join is available within the first 30 minutes and a Velos jersey is yours to keep if you complete the ride. We start and finish as a group and encourage all riders to look out for each other.

Come and try out Team Velos today and then be sure to see what other rides we host throughout the week. There is something for everybody and we guarantee a warm welcome.

Join Team Velos at. teamvelos.co.uk

Discord Channel. https://discord.gg/TDBE3FA

name: TEAM VTO POWERPUSH
description:
⚡️🚴🏼‍♂️💨 TEAM VTO - POWER PUSH! 🚴🏼‍♂️💨⚡️

Looking for a Group Ride that is different to the rest? You've found it! 👍🏻

Don't let yourself get #FOMO 😨, join us at TEAM VTO today. (www.facebook.com/groups/thenewzwifttrioz/)

Absolutely Everyone is welcome! 🙏🏻

name: Ascenders Banded Brunch Bunch Ride
description:
Welcome to [ASC] - Ascenders Brunch Bunch for a chilled Rubber Banded ride around the virtual roads of Zwift.  Each week the course will vary so whether you’re a beginner just starting out on your first group rides, this is a great way to start. If recovering from injury, or just looking for a steady paced ride then come and join us. We aim to provide a safe encouraging ride for you to enjoy. You’ll be very welcome in the Brunch Bunch


This is a banded group ride and not a race. As long as you keep spinning you will stay with the group. If you’re looking for an easy ride then sit in the group near the Yellow Lead Beacon. However if you’re feeling strong then head to the front and help pull the group along.


Please note this ride is not fenced.


Ascenders Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift <https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift>

Ascenders Discord Channel: https://discord.gg/cBfQ5jkrPh <https://discord.gg/cBfQ5jkrPh>

eventSubgroups.name: Ascenders Banded Brunch Bunch Ride (E)
eventSubgroups.description:
Welcome to [ASC] - Beanie’s Brunch Bunch for a chilled ride around the virtual roads of Zwift. Each week the course will vary so, whether you’re a beginner just starting out on your first group rides, recovering from injury, or just looking for a steady paced ride then come and join us. We aim to provide a safe encouraging ride for you to enjoy.

This is a group ride and not a race so please respect the pace and ride with the group. The fence will be used to aid regrouping and politely remind those out front to ease back to the main group. If you’re a stronger confident rider then ride behind the Lead Beacon and lend a draft for others who may need it.

PS Bring your own Brunch…

# Join Ascenders Team on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift
# Ascenders Discord Channel: https://discord.gg/cBfQ5jkrPh

name: Run 10K Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the longest running daily led running event on Zwift. This was the event that kickstarted the NoblePro Running Club in support of a world record attempt at consecutive daily10k runs.
The event has a healthy group of leaders who are friendly and chatty and will help you achieve your goal, not matter your experience. Feel free to join in the chat and become addicted like we all are.

Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it's not a race.
If you don't want to run 10k then come along for as long as you wish, the support is always appreciated.
The event runs at the same time each day, each day of the week there is a different course.

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/

https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: AusCycling Talent Identification
description:
Your journey to the top step starts in your living room.

AusCycling invites you to test yourself against the best. The workout comprises three free ride efforts: 15 seconds, 3 minutes and 12 minutes designed to test your critical power. As with any test or race, ensure you are appropriately warmed up and motivated.

The test is short and sharp, requiring maximum commitment. Erg mode will be turned off during the intervals, you are on your own and its your time to shine.

As you fade through the efforts, use your gears to reduce the resistance and maintain your cadence. Aiming for 90-100rpm for the efforts. Finish the efforts completely spent, the key is to finish all the efforts regardless of how much your power fades or how hard it feels.

Saddle up and enjoy the ride!

 Target Audience;
 16-22yo with no high-performance sport background
 People with vision or physical impairment – any age

People with background in high-performance sport – any age
*If you are outside the ages of 16-22 and have a background in high performance sport or vision/physical impairment please visit https://www.auscycling.org.au/page/virtual-tid and contact us for more information.

name: Heffron Handicap
description:
Heffron Handicap

Heffron is widely regarded as the hardest regular handicap in Australia. The quality of riders, the technical course, the history and the handicap format make Heffron the legendary race that it is. As the saying goes, “if you can win at Heffron, you can win anywhere”.

Like every Heffron, expect a blistering start, followed by settling into an uncomfortably hard rhythm and watching the other grades in the last few minutes, hoping yours is the one that takes the win. Solo or mini break a few km’s from the line or an all-out sprint with 400m to go? That’s the age-old question that Heffron asks every race. The answer? Well, that changes too often to know. Win and you can say you’ve won Heffron – and know that if you win at Heffron, you can win anywhere. Get it wrong and all you’ll be left with are the “I could have won, if only” stories we tell our mates the next day. In the end, there can only be one Heffron winner.

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: Stage 4: Zwift Games: Peaky Pave
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

name: Orange Unicorn Lite
description:
ABOUT THIS WORKOUT

Making workouts less monotonous and fun is a crucial aspect of indoor training. Over/unders is an excellent way to accomplish this. Today's workout is an efficient workout to help boost our threshold power. After a warm-up, we'll go into 2 sets of 6x (2min under and 30sec over), then cool down. You got this!

WORKOUT DESIGNER

Shayne Gaffney (GC Coaching) holds a bachelors degree in Health Science in Professional Development and Advanced Patient Care, is a USA Cycling Level 1 (expert level) Certified Coach, a level 2 certified Training Peaks coach, a USA Cycling certified power based training coach, USA Olympic Committee Safe Sport Certified, and a licensed physical therapist assistant.  He is also the creator of Zwift's "Build Me Up" Flexible Training Plan.  He can be contacted directly via  for any cycling or training related questions.

For more information on GC Coaching, please visit www.gaffneycyclingcoaching.com

ABOUT GROUP WORKOUTS

Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins. Everyone stays together regardless of power output. That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other. Only if you stop pedaling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop.

HELPFUL HINT

We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests as the estimated FTP can be inaccurate and make the difficulty level of the workouts too high for your current fitness level. 

For more info on FTP go to: https://support.zwift.com/hc/en-us/articles/210208083-What-is-FTP-and-how-is-it-used-in-Zwift

eventSubgroups.name: Orange Unicorn Lite (E)

name: PACK🐺 1.5 Beginners Ride
description:
This PACK 1.5 beginner friendly event is aimed at Zwifters looking for a group ride experience at an average lap pace of around 1.3-1.5w/kg.

On the flat, the pace will be controlled to ensure the group draft effect is kept under tight control with a target of 1.5w/kg on any hills or upslopes. The Leader will text instructions as well as use discord voice chat as communication is the key to a good group ride. Sweepers will also be in operation to assist in helping the group stay together as this is a non-drop ride.

All riders will appear in the same kit and you can optionally add (PACK) after your name to show support of the group. Please respect the pace set by the leader, this is a group ride not a race. #strongertogether

Finally, if you are just starting your zwift experience, please join our facebook group and PACK Club for tips on group riding, friendly advice and to answer any questions you have.

PACK Club:
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/Pack/join

Facebook Group #zwiftpack
https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftpack/

PACK Events:
https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/pack

https://discord.gg/cce7ers

name: TNP The No Prisoners Power Test April 5 Minute Power Challenge Week 1
description:
This is a month long challenge running throughout April, designed to test one of the most important efforts in Zwift racing.
Three Sisters Reverse Race distance: 6.9 km (lead in 3.2 km, segment length 3.7 km)
Zwift races are often won or lost in efforts around five minutes. Short climbs, hard attacks, and decisive selections usually happen in that window. If you can produce strong power for five minutes, you can stay with the front when races split. This challenge is designed to test exactly that. Hit some of Zwift’s most iconic climbs full gas and see how your best time stacks up.
"No April fools here. Just power, passion and heart. Short lead in.
One climb.
Fastest time wins.

Full gas. No prisoners."
Results will be on https://team-not-pogi-hub.vercel.app/

Fast and fun criterium races with five race times every Tuesday, Thursday, Friday, and Saturday.
A power meter or smart trainer is required for entry. A ZwiftPower account is required to be counted in the official results.
View the calendar of all Rhino Racing events: https://www.zwift.com/uk/events/tag/rhinoracing
Do you enjoy these events? We want to hear from you. Share your feedback on the Rhino Racing Discord.
Interested in team racing? We do ZRL, Dirt Racing Series, Club Ladder, and WTRL TTT events. Join us! https://discord.com/invite/wPD4HJ6

name: Triathlon Australia - Sprint Triathlon Workout
description:
Triathlon Australia welcomes all Multisport & Paratriathletes from around the world 🌍 for a 5km training run. 🏊🏼‍♂️🚴🏼‍♂️🏃🏻‍♂️🇦🇺

Your session today is written by Coach Craig Needham - Professional Development coach accredited with Triathlon Australia & 30-year veteran Multisport athlete. You will receive on-screen in-run motivation and guidance though this workout which is aimed at beginners seeking to improve their run in a Sprint Distance Triathlon (750m swim/20km ride/5km run). It is also useful for beginners seeking to make gains in Park-run style events.

We will run the Mayfield Track so you'll always see your friends close-by.


Triathlon Australia feature multiple events on Zwift each week;
📰 Latest World News on Multisports & Paratriathlon
⭐️ Special guest-star leaders and Pro's
🤨 In-Game Rides & Quizzes
🔦 Spotlight on TA squads/clubs
🎁 AWESOME PRIZE GIVEAWAYS (to claim, join our FB Group (see below)

To be involved in our community (+ claim your prize), join our FB group;

name: RCC Global Club Ride
description:
WELCOME TO THE RAPHA CYCLING CLUB WORLD WIDE GROUP RIDE. 
 
This is a social group workout; a chance every week for members to get together from around the globe, ride, chat and support each other - it’s run as a group workout session with ‘keep everyone together’ enabled so no matter what your pace you’ll stay in the pack - it’s not a race or competition and open for anyone to join us. 
We host 4 rides every week on a Tuesday to suit different time zones around the world (aimed at evenings), join whichever time works best for you. 
 
WHO IS THE RCC?
 
The Rapha Cycling Club (RCC) is, as the name suggests, Rapha’s Cycling Club. The RCC exists to help connect like minded riders from all over the world, underpinned by Rapha’s products and experiences. We ride together across continents and here united within Zwift. 
 
For more information head to: www.Rapha.cc/rcc
 
RIDE LEADERS
 
The rides are led by our friendly team of member ride leaders from around the world.  The Leader (who has a yellow beacon over their head) will ride at a steady pace as set by the workout session. Prompts will appear on screen set by the workout designer Matt Rowe so you know what to do throughout the ride. On most occasions we will have a support ride leader (who will also have a beacon), the ride leaders are there to help support you and keep the banter going - to join the chat use the Zwift Companion smartphone app. 
 
ABOUT GROUP WORKOUTS
 
Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins. Everyone stays together regardless of power output. That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other. Only if you stop pedaling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop.
 
ABOUT THIS WORKOUT

Today's workout contains 4 x 6 minute blocks of Zone 3, with some surges thrown in for good measure! The Zone 3 riding should feel nice and comfortable, however the surges may sting a little. The surges in each block are only 20 seconds long, but at 110% of FTP so require a focused increase in effort .At the end of each 6 minute block, there is a 1 minute 'Free Ride' - this is your chance to turn an endurance based session into something special! Come event day or when riding with friends, it's likely you want some more power for that final sprint to the cafe right? Give it 100% in each 60 second Free Ride section to help you become more explosive! Stress points 45: #Social
 
 
WORKOUT DESIGNER
 
Matt Rowe is the founder of Rowe & King Coaching (www.roweandking.com) with 25 years of cycling experience, coaching amateur cyclists all over the world to  achieve their personal goals.
 
HELPFUL HINT
 
We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests as the estimated FTP can be inaccurate and make the difficulty level of the workouts too high for your current fitness level.
You can adjust the difficulty of the workout mid-ride if needed, by using the up/down arrows on the left hand side, under the workout details.
 
HAVE FUN AND ENJOY THE RIDE.

#rcc

name: ZLDR AYOP Hour
description:
Start your day on the right foot or the left. Come to our 60 minute at your own pace. Run, walk, chat, laugh and get your day moving on a positive note! Attitude is 90% of any situation so let’s make this day the best day and get you in the best mindset so you can be the best you!

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!
ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

name: Stage 5: Zwift Games: Three Step Sisters | Women Only
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

Stage 5: Zwift Games: Three Step Sisters | Women Only (E)

name: Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This final session brings it all together. Two long tempo efforts build strong, sustainable endurance and reward the work you’ve done. Settle into a steady rhythm, stay composed, and finish the Alpecin–Premier Tech Training Camp strong!
