name: 3R VOLT Interval Ride [~2.6-3.2 w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. 3R VOLT runs five days a week and is designed to provide a solid and fun training platform for riders.

Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 2.8-3.2 w/kg.

The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides and training needs, the following is a typical week:
Monday – After what has hopefully been a fun backed weekend on the bike, this our recovery session
Tuesday – Tends to be more of a classic interval session, riders usually finish with an average of around 3.0w/kg
Wednesday – We take to the hills and one of our tougher sessions with riders averaging around 3.2w/kg
Thursday – is our no red beacon ride and again tough interval sets and riders finishing around 3.2w/kg
Friday - is a little different, we don't take ourselves too seriously and often play a variety of games and quizzes along side the intervals and riders end up with around 2.8-3.0w/kg

The rides are typically around an hour long, but some may take up to 75mins with the majority finishing with either a ramp or a mini race.

The group welcomes rider interaction through messaging and the VOLT discord server and features a friendly bunch of regulars to which newcomers, and riders stepping up from lower intensity rides, are always welcome. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/

This group ride is not a race so. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise.

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This final session brings it all together. Two long tempo efforts build strong, sustainable endurance and reward the work you’ve done. Settle into a steady rhythm, stay composed, and finish the Alpecin–Premier Tech Training Camp strong!

name: Cycle Nation Banded Climbing Club
description:
Want to upgrade your mountain bike or S-Works Aethos and have fun doing it? Then join us for this fun banded social ride. Every week we'll be completing a route with plenty of climbing. Just keep pedalling at your own pace and the band will keep the group together.

Most importantly this isn't a race and we ask that you respect the pace set by the ride leader which will allow for an enjoyable group ride experience for all.

name: GXY SANDBAGGERS [2.5-3.2WKG]
description:
Welcome to GXY Sandbaggers!

This is a fast paced ride where varying leaders will take you on a ‘Fast is Fun’ journey!

This is not a race and we ask that the leader’s pace and instructions are followed at all times – teamwork is essential to making this ride a success!

GXY are blessed with world class sweepers, if you need a wheel at any point, shout early and our sweepers will be on hand to support.

GXY are a friendly and supportive club who strive to help others succeed.  We are looking forward to riding with you.

If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or both, please come and join ideally via our Facebook link or by the email below:

name: Stage 4: Zwift Games: Peaky Pave
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

name: NO LIMIT CC - MIDWEEK ROLL OUT
description:
👥 No Limit Cycling Club is a community-led cycling club built around consistency, confidence, and riding together. Riders of all backgrounds and abilities are welcome.

🌤 This midweek session is designed as a short before-work ride to get the legs moving and start the day right. No drama, no pressure, just ride your ride.

🗺 Routes rotate across Zwift worlds including Watopia, France, and Makuri Islands to keep rides fresh and fun.

🧭 Ride leads manage pacing and communication. Sweepers check in with riders and help regroup when needed.

🔒 We begin with a steady warm up together before settling into a controlled group pace. Riders can push a little if they wish, or sit in and ride comfortably with the group. The focus is smooth pacing, good group riding and positive energy. Fence will be used to help keep the group together and make the ride welcoming for newer Zwift riders.**

🎽 Ride with NLCC to unlock and wear the official No Limit CC Zwift kit.

🔁 Other NLCC rides
Tue 7:00 PM GMT
Thu 7:00 PM GMT
Sat 7:00 AM GMT**

**Sun 9:05 AM GMT

📲 Contact
NLCC on Zwift: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/469e53e5-462c-4aa7-b34c-026384533c0b/home
Strava: https://strava.app.link/Vq02OaxrDZb
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/nolimit.bike**

name: Europe Morning Race
description:
Fancy a quick morning 5 km race before work? Join us each Wednesday at 6.30 AM CET (5.30 AM UTC) with 13 laps of the May Field, 5.2 km.

Choose the right category according to your speed/pace:
A: 14.0 kph+ / pace 4:10/km or faster
B: 12.0-13.99 kph / pace: 5:00/km - 4:20/km
C: 10.0-11.99 kph / pace: 5:10/km - 6:00 / km
D: 8 - 9.99 kph / pace: 6:10/km or slower

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

No problem! You can complete the run outside of Zwift. 
- Link your WTRL profile (www.wtrl.racing/profile/) to your Strava profile (Strava is free). 
- Sign-up for the Run Option through the Companion Zpp (but do not 'join the running event in-game). 
- Start and record your run on Strava within 5 minutes of finishing the bike ride, and we’ll handle the rest (your run score is usually processed within an hour).

FORMAT:
- 35 minutes of CYCLING 
- 7 minute 'Transition'
- 20 minutes of RUNNING

For full details, scoring, and registration, visit www.WTRL.racing/duathlon.

name: INC Relentless Intervals
description:
Suitable for A riders as a base session, or B & C riders wanting to push themselves.

Examples of the drills are – Z2 with 15sec Z5/6 bursts every 6minutes, alternating Z2/Z3 in various durations, Z2 - Z4 ramps of decreasing duration and many other sessions.

All sessions will be around 60-90mins and will include a warm up so just grab your bottles and head straight to the pen.

This is an Innovation Cycling Training Ride Event – all riders are welcome. These events are put together to push you further than before, if you are feeling fresh hop on the front, if you need rest surf the draft. This training ride is part of the INC RELENTLESS training rides that happen daily with each day focusing on a different area of improvement.

Tips: Use the draft to rest and ensure you do not lose the draft.

name: SZR Sunrise Ride
description:
Welcome to SZR Sunrise

This is a group ride with the focus on remaining together.
The average pace PRIOR to any race finish will be 2.2 wkg. Suitable for D riders looking to become C’s and above
Sprints and intervals will be added at the discretion of the leader.
We will at times ride above and below the advertised pace, please don’t feel the need to tell the leader they will be aware!

Please regroup quickly after sprints, intervals or hills and sit between the yellow and red beacons.
This is not a recovery ride, we work hard and work as a team.

name: Rowe & King - Wednesday Wake-up
description:
ABOUT THIS WORKOUT

Rowe & King's Workout Wednesday is a moderate intensity workout to help build and maintain your aerobic engine. A session suitable for 12 months of the year.


WORKOUT DESIGNER

Matt Rowe / Rowe & King. Ex Pro Cyclist with experience racing over Europe and European Champion on the track as a Junior in 2005 is now a qualified coach of 11 years. Rowe & King founded in 2016 is a family business with coaches Luke Rowe - Team INEOS Racer, Dani Rowe (formally King) - Olympic Champion London 2016 and Courtney Rowe accompanying Matt along with a team of additional coaches. More info at www.roweandking.com

This is part of the Workout Wednesday live broadcast series with Matt Rowe on Zwift Community Live. 
ABOUT GROUP WORKOUTS

Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins. Everyone stays together regardless of power output. That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other. Only if you stop pedaling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop.

HELPFUL HINT

We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests as the estimated FTP can be inaccurate and make the difficulty level of the workouts too high for your current fitness level. 

For more info on FTP go to: https://support.zwift.com/hc/en-us/articles/210208083-What-is-FTP-and-how-is-it-used-in-Zwift

name: ZLDR AYOP Event
description:
Join Zwift Long-Distance Runners (ZLDR) run club for a nice AYOP (at your own pace) event. Whether you want to push for a new personal best, use it as part of your weekly training program or just enjoy a sociable event this is suitable for you.

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020
Strava Club: https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020
Instagram page: https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/

https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn

Online shop: https://www.sweatydino.com

name: Bikealicious Rubberband Joy Ride
description:
This is a social ride welcoming all riders. Our mission is to push you further than before, build your endurance, and have fun. If you are feeling strong, push to the front and drag the group, if not, just sit in the peloton.

We have the “rubber-band” activated, which means that it’s a no drop ride - no matter what level you are, you will be able to keep up, and the group WILL STAY TOGETHER. Note: The overall speed may be lower than in other rides due to the banding effect.

So hop on that bike, grab those bottles, and let’s ride!

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: Le Col Cycling Club Community Sessions
description:
A structured workout designed by ex-professional cyclist, Chris Opie. The Wednesday Workout Session is all about building your FTP via a mixture of Vo2, Anaerobic and sustainable efforts. These sessions are designed to push you, but also to be achievable and constructive to your fitness, fitting in around longer weekend rides and our other sessions.
Want to know how to get more out of your cycling, nutrition or simply ask our ambassadors about their race days? This is the ride for you!
Train towards challenges, QOMs, KOMs, distance goals, participation goals and build your Zwift XP in good company.
For more information on our training sessions, head here.
https://lecol.cc/pages/events?series=train-with-pros

name: Stage 5: Zwift Games: Three Step Sisters
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: Endure IQ Training Squad Session
description:
--------------------
SESSION FOCUS
--------------------
The aim of this session is to build muscular endurance and durability at threshold. By trageting LT2 we maximally recruit fast oxidative muscle fibers which becoming too glycolytic. The aim is to develop the durability to execute longer threshold workouts.
--------------------
PRE-RIDE:
--------------------
10-Minute Glute Activation (Pre-Ride)
1. Glute Bridge – 2 x 15
2. Fire Hydrants – 10–12 each side
3. Banded Lateral Walks – 10–12 steps each way
4. Single-Leg Deadlift – 8–10 each side
5. Standing Kickbacks – 10–12 each side
Optional Finish: Pogos and 3–5 explosive skips or hops.
----------
Warm up
----------
15min easy build
----------
Main set
----------
15x60sec L4 50-60rpm 60sec easy
----------
Easy to finish
----------

eventSubgroups.name: Endure IQ Training Squad Session (E)
eventSubgroups.description:
--------------------
SESSION FOCUS
--------------------
The focus of this session is to spend as much time as possible at and above ~90% of your VO2max. The priority is on maintaining high power/pace during the work intervals. These sessions are highly stressful but great for generating adaptations that will improve your oxygen-carrying capacity and therefore top-end aerobic capacity. For long-distance triathletes, these sessions can also serve as an effective precursor to longer interval sessions.
Key physiological adaptations we are hunting today:
- Increased maximal cardiac output to push more oxygenated blood each beat
- Greater mitochondrial density so every muscle fiber can process that oxygen
- Improved muscle oxygen extraction to keep power high deep into the set
--------------------
Warm Up
--------------------
15min warm up
--------------------
Main Set
--------------------
5x30sec at FTP +30sec easy high cadence

3min L5 2min easy

2min L5 1min easy

1min L5 40sec easy

40sec L5 30sec easy

2x30sec L5 20sec easy

****3min easy between sets****

Repeat 2-3x

https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00421-020-04463-w
--------------------
Cool Down
--------------------

name: 4STAR iTT League
description:
4STAR Individual Time Trial League

It’s you versus the clock! The 4STAR Individual Time Trial League is a weekly solo challenge where riders start at set intervals and battle to post their best time. Each week features a different Zwift course — from flat, fast circuits to rolling climbs — giving you a chance to test your pacing, chase down the riders ahead, and smash new personal bests.

Push yourself, measure your progress, and see where you rank among the Zwift community. One rider. One clock. One goal — go faster every week. 🚴‍♂️💨

name: CRYO-GEN Punch Bunch
description:
The CRYO-GEN Punch Bunch is a D paced social ride with a punchy twist! We aim for a steady ~2.2 w/kg overall pace across rolling terrain and short climbs of up to ~10 minutes. Riders are welcome to hold the pace or push harder on the climbs if they’d like to battle for bragging rights. We’ll give a heads-up before setting the fence so everyone can regroup afterward, keeping the bunch together and the ride enjoyable for all.

This is a great chance for D cat riders to enjoy a social ride with some extra spice on the hills. Higher cat riders are also warmly invited—whether you’d like an easy spin with the option to test yourself on KOMs, or to lend a hand supporting the group, all are welcome!

Over the weeks we’ll take on a variety of punchy routes that promise fun, challenge, and camaraderie. All we ask is that you respect the ride leader, ride fair, and enjoy the spirit of the event. We look forward to seeing you in the bunch!

HEALTH: We would advise any rider who is attempting a Zwift ride for the first time to ensure that their health should always take precedence over completing any ride. If you feel unwell or unable to continue please STOP.

NOTE: Exercise contributes to 25% weight loss; Healthy Eating (Not Diet) contributes to the other 75%. Be fit and Healthy.


CRYOGEN Race STRAVA Club https://www.strava.com/clubs/CRYO-GEN


CRYOGEN Social STRAVA Club https://www.strava.com/clubs/474562


Team CRYO-GEN Race Facebook https://www.facebook.com/groups/176410539698267


Team CRYO-GEN Social Facebook Group https://www.facebook.com/groups/1960420317596866/

name: AERO Fit and Fun Steady State
description:
The AERO Fit and Fun Steady State is a group ride with some optional fun along the way. This is not a race so please follow the leader beacon (Yellow) instructions, if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist riders at the back of the bunch.  

All riders are welcome, we aim to create a fun, social atmosphere for your triaining and to improve your skills on Zwift.

Come join us in the green & gold (kit unlocked through completing this ride!)

Spin the legs and have a chat and if you're keen to get involved in racing in our community join us on ZwiftPower or message us via our social media accounts.

https://linktr.ee/aero.racing.team

name: WTRL Duathlon Opt3 BIKE
description:
WTRL's Duathlon League offers a unique and thrilling racing experience by combining biking on Zwift with the choice of running on Zwift or outside in real life. This makes it accessible to everyone and the ultimate test of racing versatility!

The series takes place every Wednesday year-round, allowing you to compete solo or for your club (or both!), with results and monthly leagues organized by age group, gender, and Zwift Club.

HOW DOES IT WORK?
You'll need to complete both the CYCLING and RUNNING events of the same option and complete at least 100m in each to record a score.

NO TREADMILL?
No problem! You can complete the run outside of Zwift. 
- Link your WTRL profile (www.wtrl.racing/profile/) to your Strava profile (Strava is free). 
- Sign-up for the Run Option through the Companion Zpp (but do not 'join the running event in-game). 
- Start and record your run on Strava within 5 minutes of finishing the bike ride, and we’ll handle the rest (your run score is usually processed within an hour).

FORMAT:
- 35 minutes of CYCLING 
- 7 minute 'Transition'
- 20 minutes of RUNNING

For full details, scoring, and registration, visit www.WTRL.racing/duathlon.

#wtrl #duathlon

name: PACK Run: Step by Step + After Party
description:
New to running, coming back after some time off, or simply want a nice warm up or cool down session? Join us as we spend 30 minutes moving the legs in this slow-paced, social event. The goal of the event is to encourage folks to move.

The leader will average 8kph (7:30min/km) // 5mph (12min/mile) for the duration and it is expected everyone will stay together. This is a timed event, so we encourage runners to go at their own pace if the average pace is too fast and/or use a run/walk strategy.

**Note: if you utilize a run/walk strategy, please start with the walking portion so that you are not overrunning the group on each interval.**

Put your black and orange tops and socks on and let's get moving!

Facebook Group: #zwiftpack
https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftpack/

Strava Club:
https://www.strava.com/clubs/zwift-pack-runners

Discord Voice Chat:
https://discord.gg/cce7ers

#pack

name: Rapha Women's 100 Training Ride
description:
She’s your greatest competition. Your loudest cheerleader. The one you want to beat. The one you want to win. Rapha and Zwift are celebrating best rivals and fierce friends. The women who give us a wheel to chase. A line to follow. A reason not to hit snooze. Because friendly competition pulls us forward. The harder you go, the further she’ll get.

Join us as we build up to the biggest women's participation event in cycling, the Rapha Women's 100. Returning 5th-6th September 2026.

name: Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This final session brings it all together. Two long tempo efforts build strong, sustainable endurance and reward the work you’ve done. Settle into a steady rhythm, stay composed, and finish the Alpecin–Premier Tech Training Camp strong!

name: Mountain Massif structured training: VO2 Square
description:
Created by Matt Rowe, from Rowe & King Coaching, this structured training session is designed to help you cycle at a higher intensity, and absorb surges in effort – essential attributes for any Zwift racer. 


For coaching and all your training needs visit https://roweandking.com/


About the session:
5 x 5-minutes VO2 Max Efforts, with 4 -minutes recovery between each effort.  A form finding workout!


Session details:
Example of the session to show increase in power, note this will be personalised to your FTP.


Ramp up in 10 steps:
• 1 -minute @ 140 W, Zone 1
• 1 -minute @ 158 W, Zone 1
• 1 -minute @ 177 W, Zone 2
• 1 -minute @ 195 W, Zone 2
• 1 -minute @ 214 W, Zone 2
• 1 -minute @ 236 W, Zone 3
• 1 -minute @ 254 W, Zone 3
• 1 -minute @ 273 W, Zone 3
• 1 -minute @ 291 W, Zone 4
• 1 -minute @ 310 W, Zone 4

Recovery:
• 3 -minutes @ 124 W, Zone 1

Repeat Intervals x 5:

• Hard, 5-minutes @ 341 W, Zone 5
• Easy, 4-minutes @ 124 W, Zone 1

Cool down:
• 2-minutes @ 124 W, Zone 1

Safety Note:
Only undertake vigorous exercise if you are fit and healthy to do so. If you are at all unsure, consult a Physician or Doctor first.  All training equipment including Bikes and Turbo trainers should be in full working order. Do not use any piece of equipment that does not come up to the required standard.


More information:
For more information check out the Mountain Massif website www.mountainmassif.com, follow on Facebook, Instagram (Mountain_Massif) and join our Zwift Club by clicking the following link https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b639575d-930f-4de6-a79d-bea3313350bc/join.  To record all your training data and chart your progress, consider using the TrainingPeaks app (www.trainingpeaks.com/).

name: Aussie Crit Crushers Race 1
description:
Aussie Crit Crushers Race is a fun short sharp hitout that consists of two events back to back. When signing up for Race #1 make sure to register for race #2.

Race #1 is on Crit City -Downtown Dolphin ( x4 laps)
Race #2 is on New York -LaGuardia Loop ( x4 laps)

Jump in race both events to be honored winning the XP Cup!
This is a graded Crit race, yes you can crush your mates, whilst also crushing your own PR times each week! 
Com’on what are you waiting for SIGN UP your mates are waiting for you to join them!

name: Stage 6a: Zwift Games: Epiloch
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

name: QueenBee Wellness Wednesday
description:
The QueenBees are a group of women who share a love of cycling. Just as a hive works together to bring out the best in every member of the community, so do we. We strive to support, empower and encourage women to live their best lives, no matter where they are on their life, cycling or fitness journey. 



Wellness Wednesday is a social ride aimed at beginners, pre/rehab, those looking for recovery or a low watt ride. Everyone of all abilities are welcome with a social pace around 1.0 - 1.3 wpkg. During the ride try to stay with the yellow lead beacon and follow instructions from your ride leader.



Join our QueenBee hive here (ladies only):

https://www.facebook.com/groups/218570110355444

description:

name: Flanders Endurance Series
description:
Flanders Endurance Series is focused on long distance rides/races.
The global distance of these fondo's will always be around 100km or 2.30h depending on the course of the day.
Use these type of rides to build up your foundation and/or stamina ...
Do you have to finish the ride ? No, that’s not necessary, only when you want to end up in the results you have to do the « whole » ride !
Is this a race ? There will be results displayed for those who like to race it, but you can also tackle it as a great build-me-up endurance training.
Late join is possible.

Heart rate monitors are necessary when you want to end up in the ZP results !

It’s also a great opportunity for you to earn those missing ROUTE badges

#keepmoving #BZReCyclingClub #jerseyunlock

Spotify playlist : BZR`s FTP Booster
Facebook Group : https://m.facebook.com/groups/bzrecyclingclub/

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Champs Elysees
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Workout Wednesday Run
description:
Join the Zwift Runners for a group run workout that will be of benefit to all distance runners.

The speeds for each group are based on a threshold speed of A group 15 kph, B 13 kph, C 11 kph, D 9 kph. We will start the intervals at that speed but increase the speed of the intervals we get into the workout. We encourage runners to choose the group closest to the effort level you want to run at and to keep to the nominal speed for that group to maintain the group dynamic. However if these speeds are significantly too low or high for you then you can choose to run in the group that is closest and then adjust the target speed up to 10% up or down in the game or via the Companion app.

If you have any questions about this event or want to find out more about Zwift Running in general then please visit the Zwift Runners Facebook group or zwift.com/run #gozwiftrun #wow

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: WKG Jungle Down Under.
description:
Come join us for a 1 lap spin on the dusty gravel roads of the Mayan Jungle in Watopia.

We will be shredding the Serpentine8 Route, celebrating everything off road, All riders will be assigned a Canyon Grail gravel bike which makes this a fast dusty ride!

Riders will start behind the fence and ride as a group for the lead-in and a circuit of the jungle, when we reach the start/finish Arch for the 2nd time the fence will be removed and it is a 10km race to the finish.

Please check out WKG on Facebook for our events and community information.

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Glasgow Crit Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: WTRL Duathlon Opt3 RUN
description:
WTRL's Duathlon League offers a unique and thrilling racing experience by combining biking on Zwift with the choice of running on Zwift or outside in real life. This makes it accessible to everyone and the ultimate test of racing versatility!

The series takes place every Wednesday year-round, allowing you to compete solo or for your club (or both!), with results and monthly leagues organized by age group, gender, and Zwift Club.

HOW DOES IT WORK?
You'll need to complete both the CYCLING and RUNNING events of the same option and complete at least 100m in each to record a score.

NO TREADMILL?
No problem! You can complete the run outside of Zwift. 
- Link your WTRL profile (www.wtrl.racing/profile/) to your Strava profile (Strava is free). 
- Sign-up for the Run Option through the Companion Zpp (but do not 'join the running event in-game). 
- Start and record your run on Strava within 5 minutes of finishing the bike ride, and we’ll handle the rest (your run score is usually processed within an hour).

FORMAT:
- 35 minutes of CYCLING 
- 7 minute 'Transition'
- 20 minutes of RUNNING

For full details, scoring, and registration, visit www.WTRL.racing/duathlon.

#wtrl #duathlon

name: WTRL Duathlon Opt4 BIKE
description: WTRL's Duathlon League offers a unique and thrilling racing experience by combining biking on Zwift with the choice of running on Zwift or outside in real life. This makes it accessible to everyone and the ultimate test of racing versatility!

name: Aussie AHDR p/b Giant
description:
Join one of Oz’s largest and fastest growing group rides.  It’s a moderate 2.3-2.8 w/kg group ride, not a race.  OK, there is fun race at the end called the "after party".  Wait for the leader to announce when it starts.  Results will be displayed when you cross the finish line. Be sure to turn up the volume to hear the pre-ride audio instructions from ride leader Tim Searle. Over 200 Zwifters took part in past rides so come join the fun!


#ahdr

name: Ascenders Brunch Bunch Ride
description:
Welcome to [ASC] - Beanie's Brunch Bunch for a chilled ride around the virtual roads of Zwift. Each week the course will vary so, whether you're a beginner just starting out on your first group rides, recovering from injury, or just looking for a steady paced ride then come and join us. We aim to provide a safe encouraging ride for you to enjoy. You’ll be very welcome in the Brunch Bunch

This is a group ride and not a race so please respect the pace and ride with the group. The fence will be used to aid regrouping and politely remind those out front to ease back to the main group before the 60 second countdown ends. If you're a stronger confident rider then ride behind the Lead Beacon and lend a draft for others who may need it.

Ascenders Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwiftOpensOpens a new window a new window

Ascenders Discord Channel: https://discord.gg/cBfQ5jkrPh

name: Run 10K Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the longest running daily led running event on Zwift. This was the event that kickstarted the NoblePro Running Club in support of a world record attempt at consecutive daily10k runs.
The event has a healthy group of leaders who are friendly and chatty and will help you achieve your goal, not matter your experience. Feel free to join in the chat and become addicted like we all are.

Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it's not a race.
If you don't want to run 10k then come along for as long as you wish, the support is always appreciated.
The event runs at the same time each day, each day of the week there is a different course.

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/

https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Rave Down Under
description:
It’s time to heal those dancing injuries from all the raving or the sore muscles from all the racing.   Join Team RAVE for a recovery ride with the fellas of down under.  This is not a race but feel free to challenge yourself or take it easy.
The yellow beacon will cruise at 1.5 w/kg
The red beacon at 1.0 w/kg .
Find a group that suits you, blob up but above all, HAVE FUN! That’s all that matters.

name: Team Italy Workout Series by Manuel Ferrari
description:
Welcome to the Team Italy High-Intensity WORKOUT Series, written by Manuel Ferrari (Manuel Bike Experience) open to all riders with the aim of improving your FTP and preparing for the next races.

The appointment will be for every Tuesday and Thursday evening and is connected with the workouts that we offer every Wednesday evening during the Team Italy Up and Down Intervals event.

Have you always dreamed of completing a ride of over 100 km? Participating in both Team Italy Up and Down Intervals events and today's workout on consecutive days will improve your endurance and transform your ride of over 100 km into a fun ride.

Please add the [ITA] tag after your name,
Your kit will be automatically selected, and all riders will appear with the same kit.
All riders will unlock our exclusive in-game kit at the end of the workout.

How the ERG workout mode works:
The Zwifters line up in the starting area like a normal event and once it starts the workout mode begins. Each one will stay together with the group regardless of the power supplied, calculated on the basis of your FTP current.

We strongly recommend that you perform one of the FTP tests, if this is not correctly estimated it could make the workout too demanding for your current level of preparation, or ineffective. For more information about FTP go to: https://support.zwift.com/hc/en-us/articles/210208083-What-is-FTP-and-how-is-it-used-in-Zwift
This is the Facebook group of Team Italy
https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftitaly
and Strava club of Team Italy
https://www.strava.com/clubs/zwift_italy
This is the Manuel Bike Experience Youtube channel where you can relive some Workout and other interesting related topics:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCvkG00ZD9Td2LXMKkeu1R3Q
Join us! Have fun! Ride On!
#teamitaly #jerseyunlock

eventSubgroups.name: Team Italy Workout Series by Manuel Ferrari (E)

name: TEAM ITALY From Walk to Run 5K
description:
WELCOME TO ALL RUNNERS! 5Km running is your goal? This is a Run-Walk-Run event, that which will help you to start running, to return after an injury, to increase your training or simply to train for weelness. The event is structured as workouts to give you the opportunity to manage the paces according to your personal pace references for a functional aerobic improvement training. Each workout will include initial walking warm up, so please set your treadmills to exit the starting pen all in group walking and follow the subsequent messages on the screen. The Running Division of Team Italy is a new reality that is added to the already more acclaimed Bike Division. It was born from the passion between two runner friends, zwift users, and boss of Team, which we thanks. So, we hope you enjoy with us. Also in the other events, which you can find on our Facebook page. Please, add tag [ITA] after your name. Stay connected with other runners via our Team Italy Discord Server (Running/Corsa Section - Audio Channel) https://discord.gg/c7PGKGv

This is the Facebook group of Team Italy Runners https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftrunningitaly/

 , and Team Italy https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftitaly

 Join us! Have fun! Run On!. #zwiftrunningitaly #teamitaly Benvenuti a tutti i Runners ! Il vostro obiettivo è quello di correre 5km?Questo è un evento Corsa-Camminata-Corsa, che potrà aiutarvi ad iniziare a correre, a rientrare dopo un infortunio, ad incrementare il vostro allenamento o semplicemente per il benessere. L’evento è strutturato come workouts per darvi la possibilità di gestire le andature secondo i vostri personali riferimenti di passo per un allenamento funzionale di miglioramento aerobico. Ogni workouts prevedrà un riscaldamento camminando, quindi vi preghiamo di impostare i vostri Tapis Roulant per uscire dalla griglia di partenza tutti in gruppo camminando e seguire a video i successivi messaggi. La Divisione Running del Team Italy è una recente realtà aggiunta alla già acclamata divisione Cycling. Nata dalla passione di due amici runners, utenti Zwift, ed il Boss del Team Italy, che ringraziamo. Grazie anche a voi, ed alla vostra passione questa realtà sta crescendo e divenendo sempre più attiva. Per tutte le info necessarie potete trovare la nostra pagina Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftrunningitaly/

 La pagina del Team Italy: https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftitaly

 ed il canale Discord, Running audio channel, per tenerci in contatto: https://discord.gg/c7PGKGv

eventSubgroups.name: TEAM ITALY From Walk to Run 5K (E)

name: ZLDR One Step At A Time
description:
Join Zwift Long-Distance Runners (ZLDR) run club for a relaxed 7.5k run. Whether you want to use this time to walk just to get your blood flowing or push for a new personal best or anything in between, this is a great session to join! Even if you can’t join for the full 7.5k, come for whatever you have available. We are a flexible group of walkers/runners who want to help you get consistent and get results.

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020
Strava Club: https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020
Instagram page: https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/
Discord Invitation (not required for the run)
https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn

name: Stage 6b: Zwift Games: Crit Cade
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

name: ZZRC Tea & Chat Banded Ride
description:
Welcome to ZZRC Tea & Chat Banded Ride 

This ride is perfect for riders who want the more social aspect of riding, than the watts being pushed . 

We are here for a good old ride and chat , and our yellow beacons love to do that . 

So regardless of your fitness , join us for the casual chat and enjoy the zwift scenery on route

Don’t forget to follow ZZRC on Facebook, Instagram , Tik Tok  and our fab website . 

We will be using the 1.5 discord channel if you want to chat there 

 https://discord.gg/ZTJbYAn8

name: HISP- The Route of the Week
description:
A race with HISP plus some free XPs? Can’t get any better! Every week we’ll compete on the Route of the Week, with three time slots a day, all part of a monthly league (4 stages)
⚠️ IMPORTANT: to claim your XPs, make sure you’ve clicked on the Route of the Week banner on Zwift’s main page and joined the challenge previous to the Race.
The league’s overall standings will follow ZRS and will be available on ZwiftPower.


¿Una carrera con HISP más unos XPs Gratis? ¡Mejor imposible! Todas las semanas competiremos en la ruta de la semana, con tres horarios al día, en una liga mensual (4 etapas)
⚠️ IMPORTANTE: recuerda que para recibir los XP tienes que haber accedido al banner Ruta de la Semana en la página principal de Zwift para inscribirte al desafío antes de la carrera.
La clasificación general de la liga se regirá por ZRS y estará disponible en ZwiftPower.

name: PACK🐺 Social Endurance
description:
Looking for a longer midweek social ride across a variety of routes? Well Join the PACK for a 90 minute ride to help build your endurance and still have a friendly chat along the way.

Pace will be 2w/kg on the flats and rolling roads with 2.1w/kg on any climbs. Any larger climbs might have a regroup at the top so please follow the leader’s instructions.

All riders will appear in the same kit (unlocked at the finish) and you can optionally add (PACK) after your name to show support of the group. Please respect the pace set by the leader, this is a group ride not a race. #strongertogether

Finally, if you are just starting your zwift experience, join our facebook group for tips on group riding, friendly advice and to answer any questions you have.

PACK Club:
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/Pack/join

Facebook Group #zwiftpack
https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftpack/

PACK Events:
https://zwift.com/events/tag/pack

Discord Voice Chat:
https://discord.gg/cce7ers

Zwiftpower Team:
https://www.zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=2928

name: Crit Cade Steering Race
description:
Steering Required: This event is designed to maximize the fun of Zwift Steering! If you have a steering device, now's the time to master those apexes and gain an advantage. If you do not have a steering device, unfortunately you will not be able to join.

Get ready for an electrifying dash under the lights in the Crit Cade Steering Racing Experience! This isn't your average crit—it's 8 laps of pure, high-octane racing Downtown Dolphin circuit, but with a thrilling arcade twist!

Power Up Pandemonium: This race features Power Ups! Strategically deploy your Boost Power ups to cut through the field. Timing is everything! Each Boost power up will give you a 10kph speed increase for 2 seconds!

Speed Boosts Galore: Keep your eyes peeled for additional Speed Boosts scattered across the course to give you that extra injection of pace when you need it most. But watch out because these boosts can move around, so you’ll need to be on top of your steering game!

Steering Required: This event is designed to maximize the fun of Zwift Steering! If you have a steering device, it’s the time to master those apexes and gain an advantage.

These events are held within our Zwift Labs club. This feature is still in development and we'd love your feedback on your experience.

name: Grimpeur Giant
description:
The race is presented by Team KST, Denmark.
This event is designed so you can go “full throttle” on a climb for a shortened period of time and to push yourself beyond your perceived limits.

name: Mighty Hilarious Climbers
description:
Mighty Hilarious Climbers - a relaxed quad massage up the alp or a crazy climb - your choice as it own paced no fences. This is not a race, just a friendly ride. 

Our Obsessed Captain The Flying Gerbil is welcoming you!

It’s all about the climb and conversation with shortness breath, not the time.
Good chatting guaranteed!

Join our Coffee Lovers Club in Strava, Zwift and Facebook for more information and communication!

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: WTRL Duathlon Opt4 RUN
description: WTRL's Duathlon League offers a unique and thrilling racing experience by combining biking on Zwift with the choice of running on Zwift or outside in real life. This makes it accessible to everyone and the ultimate test of racing versatility!

name: TNP The No Prisoners Power Test April 5 Minute Power Challenge Week 1
description:
This is a month long challenge running throughout April, designed to test one of the most important efforts in Zwift racing.
Three Sisters Reverse Race distance: 6.9 km (lead in 3.2 km, segment length 3.7 km)
Zwift races are often won or lost in efforts around five minutes. Short climbs, hard attacks, and decisive selections usually happen in that window. If you can produce strong power for five minutes, you can stay with the front when races split. This challenge is designed to test exactly that. Hit some of Zwift’s most iconic climbs full gas and see how your best time stacks up.
"No April fools here. Just power, passion and heart. Short lead in.
One climb.
Fastest time wins.

Full gas. No prisoners."
Results will be on https://team-not-pogi-hub.vercel.app/

name: CRYO-GEN & ZLDR Wednesday Workout RUN
description:
Join our CRYO-GEN & ZLDR Wednesday Workout Run. It is our collaborative event between CRYO-GEN and ZLDR. The goals are to run together, share the FUN and stay healthy as one zwift run community. We do workout every Wednesday, feel free to join and have FUN on your mid week. You can set the bias up or down if needed too.

To find out more about Team CRYO-GEN and what we can offer you, please visit our website https://cryogen.team/

If you wish to join us on Discord for this run, please join our server here - https://discord.gg/MkEpFwNz

name: Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This final session brings it all together. Two long tempo efforts build strong, sustainable endurance and reward the work you’ve done. Settle into a steady rhythm, stay composed, and finish the Alpecin–Premier Tech Training Camp strong!

name: DIRT 505 Wednesday Endurance Ride
description:
Welcome to the DIRT 505 Wednesday Endurance Ride!

Please join us weekly on Wednesdays at a pace of 2.6-2.8w/kg for 90 minutes.

The 505 rides take place Monday, Wednesday, Thursday and Friday for 90 minutes at the same time and same consistent pace. Saturday's we ride for 120 minutes.

We kick off our rides with a 5 minute pace at 2.2w/kg to get us warmed up and together as a team. The fence is used throughout the ride to keep everyone together. The fence will not kick you off the ride but we do encourage all our riders to stay together as close to the beacon as possible. Our rides offer a 30 minute late join option.
The ride leader will stay as close to ride pace as possible. We try to change up the routes each week and some of the routes may include some small to moderate climbs.

Our group exists to encourage and build up cyclists around the world, no matter your time zone, fitness level, pro or amateur. We exist to encourage and build up a team culture of unity in the cycling community.

STRAVA DIRT CLUB:

https://www.strava.com/clubs/zwiftdirt

FACEBOOK DIRT GROUP:

https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftdirt

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Champs Elysees
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Wednesday Waddle with Afterparty p/b Ascenders Team
description:
Join us for 30 or 60 minutes. We will warm up for 10mins @ 5:15k pace before doing some intervals with some well timed recovery. We will adjust the pace of the recovery to keep the majority of the group together.
 
 10 mins @ 5:15k
 5 mins @ 5:10
 5 mins @ 5min pace
 2 mins easy @ 5:15
 5 mins@ 4:30
 5 mins @ 4:45
 2 mins @ 5:20
 5 mins @ 4:30
 2 mins @ 5:20
 8 mins @ 4:30
 2 mins @ 5:20
 5 mins @ 4:40
 4 mins cool down @ 5:25
 
 Join the Ascenders Team FB group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift

#ascenders

name: Workout Wednesday Run
description:
Join the Zwift Runners for a group run workout that will be of benefit to all distance runners.

The speeds for each group are based on a threshold speed of A group 15 kph, B 13 kph, C 11 kph, D 9 kph. We will start the intervals at that speed but increase the speed of the intervals we get into the workout. We encourage runners to choose the group closest to the effort level you want to run at and to keep to the nominal speed for that group to maintain the group dynamic. However if these speeds are significantly too low or high for you then you can choose to run in the group that is closest and then adjust the target speed up to 10% up or down in the game or via the Companion app.

If you have any questions about this event or want to find out more about Zwift Running in general then please visit the Zwift Runners Facebook group or zwift.com/run #gozwiftrun #wow

name: Team ODZ Coffee & Espresso
description:
The ODZ Coffee Ride is a group ride formatted to allow a steady 2.5w/kg warmup, followed by an all out ‘Espresso segment’.  Riders must be BEHIND rider leader at the start of the ‘Espresso segment’ to be included in final results.  Watch in messaging closely for instructions during ride.  

Save ride with ODZ and (Category) after race. i.e. T. Zwift ODZ Coffee (A). Go to http://www.teamodz.com/coffee-ride/ for a full description.

A: 4.0 W/kg FTP or higher 
B: 3.2 W/kg to 3.99 W/kg FTP 
C: 2.5 W/kg to 3.19 W/kg FTP 
D: Under 2.49 W/kg FTP

#usacycling #odz

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: Zwift Epic Race - Downtown Dolphin
description:
Join us as we take on some slightly more challenging race routes!

For more Epic Races, please join the club on the Companion App: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/a1ea0324-7f4a-46a7-8547-a7a3246e349b/home

All racers will start together, so make sure you're ready at the start!

WHAT IS ZWIFTPOWER? 
For further information on connecting to ZwiftPower, check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note: For your results to show up in ZwiftPower, you must use a heart rate monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter. 

Check out zwift.com/racing for more information on how you can participate in other events offered by Zwift and the community to have a great racing experience.

#zwiftofficial

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Glasgow Crit Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

description: WTRL's Duathlon League offers a unique and thrilling racing experience by combining biking on Zwift with the choice of running on Zwift or outside in real life. This makes it accessible to everyone and the ultimate test of racing versatility!

name: Bikealicious Highnoon-Heroes
description:
It’s high noon… and the real heroes are on their bikes.

Join the Bikealicious Highnoon-Heroes every Wednesday for one glorious hour of midday spin therapy.



This is a Banded Ride, which means:



Nobody gets dropped

Nobody has to chase

Everybody stays together like a well-oiled peloton sandwich



Expect an easy, recovery-friendly pace, perfect for shaking out tired legs, escaping the lunch break, or just pretending you’re working while Zwifting instead. Chat, laugh, spin — watts are optional, good vibes are mandatory.

eventSubgroups.description:

Join the Bikealicious Highnoon-Heroes every Wednesday at 12:00 CET for one glorious hour of midday spin therapy.

This is a Banded Ride, which means:

Nobody gets dropped
Nobody has to chase
Everybody stays together like a well-oiled peloton sandwich

Expect an easy, recovery-friendly pace, perfect for shaking out tired legs, escaping the lunch break, or just pretending you’re working while Zwifting instead. Chat, laugh, spin — watts are optional, good vibes are mandatory.

name: Xert Surplus Seeker Ride
description:
This is a friendly group ride for users of Xert, want-to-be-users of Xert, or anyone from around the world who’d like to ride Wednesdays in a social, friendly setting. Difficulty is low as we will aim to ride together at a pace of 2.0-2.5 W/kg every week to build XSS (strain). The ride will be hosted by the team at Xert so while we’re primarily here to meet riders in the best way we know how - from the saddles of our bikes -  we’ll happily answer questions about the system (oxygen availability depending!). We will do our best to keep the group together so please keep your eyes open for a virtual tug on the jersey pocket (messages)..

Our Discord voice channel can be found @ https://discord.gg/Uqkd2Rx

name: KISS at Base Training Ride
description:
Join us to work on your endurance and power output at your aerobic threshold (AT) in Zone 2. The exercise will give you endurance and increased performance using fat as energy. A well-developed AT can give you better race performance increase the amount of time before your body uses glycogen stores.

Our Wednesday ride starts with a mass start. Pick the category you think you belong in and if you want to go harder or easier during the ride you can float between groups. All groups ride the same course and are visible.

We are now offering four separate paced rides to better accommodate various fitness levels.
The A cat is for riders with a Zone 2 above 3.80 w/kg.
The B cat is for riders with Zone 2 between 3.2 - 3.80 w/kg.
The C cat is for riders with Zone 2 between 2.5 - 3.19 w/kg.
The D cat is for riders with Zone 2 between 2.2 - 2.49 w/kg.
Remember this when you approach hills and inclines along the way. Stick together to enjoy the draft and companionship. The ride leader will not exceed the advertised pace at any time - even on hills. Please keep an eye on the leader beacon (yellow) and the sweeper beacon (red) to stay in the blob.

So turn on your graph (press G on computer keyboard or select it from on-screen menu in iOS and Android) and paint it blue. Give the body a good foundation for Zwift racing. Discord server Link: https://discord.gg/kBmXhY2

Wednesday's feature a bit of climbing to spice up your base training, I am sure you will enjoy it.

name: GRIT Partytime
description:
Join us for our weekly ride/race.
As always this is a group ride. We will start off together with an easy warm up around 2.5wpk.
The pace will pick up for the first few laps but the goal is to stay together. Listen to the leader for instructions.

The last lap or two will be a race. It is key to stay in the group for the start of party time.
Come party with us.

eventSubgroups.name: GRIT Partytime  (B)
eventSubgroups.description: Come join the party, click here to join.

name: 3R Endurance Ride
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that no riders ride off the front of the group. The event is designed as part of 3R’s package of #ProStyleTraining events to assist with the attainment of your training goals and may include intervals over and below the ride average parameters.
This is not a race so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack.
We welcome good banter and aim to create a fun, social atmosphere for your training. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief

#Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise
#3r

name: JETT Sub 2.5w/kg Enjoy Home Japan Ride
description:
Welcome to JETT EnjoyHome Japan Ride !
Is this event a race? -> No, it's not.
This event is a group ride. Yellow Beacon leads the group on a steady ride at 2.0~2.5w/kg. Attendees are encouraged to ride without leaving the Yellow Beacon.
You can also chat each other to make the ride more fun.

Power : 2.0~2.5w/kg
Double Draft : ON

===

このイベントはグループライドです。イエロービーコンは2.0~2.5w/kg で安定したライドでグループをLeadします。参加者の皆さんは、Yellow Beaconから離れないでライドしましょう。
また、ライダー同士でチャットにより交流をして、楽しいライドにしましょう。

Power : 2.0~2.5w/kg
Double Draft : ON

#jett

name: SWX Wednesday Tempo by Coach Cindy 🇸🇬🇯🇵🇦🇺
description:
Build strength and control every Wednesday with SWX Tempo — a steady endurance builder designed to grow your aerobic engine.
Crafted by Coach Cindy (Train Smart = Race Stronger), this session focuses on sustained tempo efforts to improve muscular endurance, pacing, and long-ride efficiency. Bring smooth cadence, mental focus, and your best zone 3 vibes!

• All levels welcome
• Stay-together mode enabled
• Late join allowed

Connect and learn more about who we are:
📸 Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/shark_worx_swx
🌐 Website: https://bit.ly/Visit_Coach_Cindy

eventSubgroups.name: SWX Wednesday Tempo by Coach Cindy 🇸🇬🇯🇵🇦🇺 (E)

name: Cryo-Gen Humpday Hussle
description:
The Cryo-Gen Humpday Hussle has been organized for the many
riders in the Zwift Community who wish to have a paced ride with friends and
like-minded members of the community. The ride has not been organized as a
workout ride but as a social gathering with a mini race at the end. The average
pace will be C & D pace. All riders are encouraged to stick
with the yellow beacon. We are stronger when we work together. Stronger riders
are welcome to assist slower riders back to the pack if they fall off. This
should be a fun, relaxed ride to help you get you ready for your next big
effort, whether it’s a workout, race, or a climb.

CRYOGEN Race STRAVA Club https://www.strava.com/clubs/CRYO-GEN

name: Rapha Women's 100 Training Ride
description:
She’s your greatest competition. Your loudest cheerleader. The one you want to beat. The one you want to win. Rapha and Zwift are celebrating best rivals and fierce friends. The women who give us a wheel to chase. A line to follow. A reason not to hit snooze. Because friendly competition pulls us forward. The harder you go, the further she’ll get.

Join us as we build up to the biggest women's participation event in cycling, the Rapha Women's 100. Returning 5th-6th September 2026.
